title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man allegedly wielding a sword during a mental health crisis near Northeastern University.
The man attacked officers, stabbing one in the arm; police fired tasers and weapons in response.
Multiple officers and two EMS responders suffered non-life-threatening injuries; police and DA's office are investigating.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer.
She aims to support the Cancer Care Equity program at MGH, which helps families cover costs during cancer treatment.
Dubois qualified for the marathon in September 2025 and is running with the Massachusetts General Hospital team.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A Brockton house fire killed one woman and hospitalized two others early Saturday morning.
Fire broke out at 80 Glendale Ave. around 12:30 a.m., with intense flames on the second floor.
Officials are still investigating the fire's cause, but it does not seem suspicious or intentional.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was killed by police in Raymond, NH, after allegedly shooting at officers.
Masse previously wounded a Nottingham officer after firing at family members, leading to a police search.
Police found Masse in Raymond woods; a gunfire exchange led to his death, with no other injuries reported.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon, a 47-year-old from Canton, diagnosed with breast cancer in 2024, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Her best friend Peggy, also diagnosed with cancer and a widow of sarcoma, will join her in the race.
Both women will run the marathon in support of Mass General Hospital, advocating cancer survivorship and friendship.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' on April 4, marking his fifth time hosting the show.
Jack White was the musical guest, performing for the fifth time, including one appearance with The White Stripes.
Black's episode included a cameo-filled sketch with fellow 'five-timers' Jonah Hill and Tina Fey.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown overcame past left-hand criticisms due to injuries, improving his ability to play with his left side.
Brown had major wrist surgery in 2021 and reinjured it in 2023, affecting his left-hand performance.
Brown's improved health has led to career highs: 28.7 points, 7.0 rebounds, 5.3 assists per game this season.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo, former Red Sox announcer, returns to Boston with the San Diego Padres, now his broadcasting team.
Orsillo reflects on his time with NESN, calling it a 'dream job' despite an abrupt end over a decade ago.
Reuniting with former colleagues, Orsillo shares his appreciation for fans and his new role with the Padres.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora used Roman Anthony as designated hitter due to his defensive struggles.
Roman Anthony contributed offensively with a triple and a hit in the game against the Padres.
Cora defended Anthony's improved throwing angle despite an error allowing the Padres to lead 3-2.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early allowed three hits and four walks in four innings against the Padres despite struggling with control.
Early held the Padres to two runs, and the Red Sox lost 3-2 after Aroldis Chapman allowed the winning run.
In his MLB career, Early has 39 strikeouts and has allowed no home runs in 28.2 innings pitched.
